commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Have mercy upon me, O LORD; for I am weak: O LORD, heal me; for my bones are troubled. Troubled, with grief. (Worthington) I am sinking under my illness: my virtue is lost. (Calmet) The whole human race is this sick man, requiring the aid of Jesus Christ. Augustine) The ineffable name Jehova, (Haydock) is repeated thrice, to insinuate that salvation must come from the Blessed Trinity. (Berthier, ver. 9) Under the allegory of sickness, the ravages of sin appear. (Menochius).
